title: Dillenia membranifolia
intro: Dillenia membranifolia, also known as the paper dillenia, is a plant species belonging to the family Sarcolenaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of D. membranifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Dillenia membranifolia is classified within the family Dilleniaceae. This family is a small group of flowering plants, comprising about 200 species. The genus Dillenia itself is native to tropical Asia, and Dillenia membranifolia is a species within this genus. Dillenia membranifolia is further classified as a member of the order Dilleniales, which is a distinct and ancient lineage of flowering plants. Its classification reflects its unique evolutionary history and distinct characteristics within the plant kingdom.
